content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Code-Switching Prompts for Enhanced Multilingual and Low-Resource LLM Performance",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) often underperform on multilingual and especially low-resource language tasks, struggling to leverage cross-lingual knowledge and context. Existing prompting methods are static and typically monolingual, failing to exploit natural multilingual phenomena like code-switching.",
  "motivation": "Language contact and code-switching are prevalent in real-world multilingual communication, yet LLMs are rarely prompted to utilize these phenomena. Harnessing code-switching in prompts could guide LLMs to better transfer knowledge between high-resource and low-resource languages, improving performance in underrepresented languages and dialects. Addressing this gap is crucial as LLM deployment expands globally.",
  "method": "We propose a novel dynamic code-switching prompting strategy: for any given low-resource language task, prompts are automatically constructed to alternate between target low-resource language and a related high-resource language at syntactic or semantic boundaries. This method leverages bilingual dictionaries and typological similarity data to insert high-resource phrases or context where LLM uncertainty is highest (as assessed by token-level entropy on the prompt), encouraging the model to anchor its reasoning in both languages. Prompt generation is adaptive, using a gating mechanism conditioned on LLM confidence and external language models for code-switch timing.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on multilingual QA and translation benchmarks (e.g., FLORES-200, XQuAD) focusing on 10+ low-resource languages. Baselines include monolingual prompts, translation-augmented prompts, and previous cross-lingual prompting methods. Metrics will include BLEU, F1, and exact match, as well as code-switch-aware robustness and transferability measures."
}
